planner.reason: qwen3.7-max timed out and kimi-k3 returned HTTP 400 after a local budget-gate retry with compressed input

objective: Make quality_target a shared, fail-closed minimum role-quality floor while choosing the lowest reliable cost among models that satisfy it.

success_criteria.quality_floor: draft, production, audit, and frontier enforce minimum role bands 2, 3, 4, and 4; lower or unregistered models do not enter role routes
success_criteria.cost_order: after the quality floor, order by route health, budget eligibility, free status, retry-adjusted cost, P95 latency, and quality surplus
success_criteria.surface_consistency: recommend_route, route_plan, build_workflow_plan, and run_llm_task share one role-policy helper
success_criteria.tests: focused tests plus the complete suite, compileall, and git diff --check pass
success_criteria.budget: total model cost stays below USD 0.05 and failed early gates stop later calls
success_criteria.security: no credentials or private user data enter artifacts and Hermes unattended safety is unchanged

eligibility: Role tasks admit only models explicitly registered in ROLE_QUALITY_BANDS at or above the target floor.
A role task never falls back to an unregistered general-pool model when no qualified role model exists.
Existing privacy, modality, cooldown, provider entitlement, and max-cost gates remain hard constraints.
ordering: non-degraded route before degraded route when enough task-specific health evidence exists
higher quality surplus above the required floor
existing stable role order as the penultimate tie-breaker only when every earlier key ties
provider priority as the final deterministic tie-breaker only when every earlier key ties

tests: draft verification selects the registered band-2 trial route and excludes unregistered general models
production verification rejects the band-2 route and selects a band-3-or-higher route
frontier planning rejects band-3 routes even when they are free
production may select a healthy free band-3 route ahead of a paid band-4 route because both meet the floor
recommend, route-plan, workflow-plan, and run_llm_task expose the same floor and selection result
a direct run_llm_task integration test selects the same qualified route as planning and raises a clear runtime failure when no route meets the floor
existing degraded-route, privacy, budget, family-independence, and full-suite tests remain green
no qualified role route returns an empty recommendation or clear runtime failure without falling back to an unregistered general model
existing role order and provider priority remain deterministic final tie-breakers when all earlier sort keys tie
risks: Changing the sort order can alter production model choices; focused route-order tests and live dry-runs are required.
A strict floor can leave no eligible route; this is intentional fail-closed behavior and must be visible in output.
Free trial routes can disappear; health and cooldown remain ahead of free preference.
rollback: Remove the quality-target floor map and quality_target parameter from the role-policy helper.
Restore the previous highest-band-first sort and production-only role-policy call sites.
Keep the public workflow artifacts as evidence even if code is rolled back.
acceptance: quality_floor, cost_order, surface_consistency, tests, budget, and security all pass.
No new dependency, service, credential, or model promotion is introduced.
The complete project test suite, compileall, and git diff --check pass.
A final independent verifier judges the implementation against the original contract.
